crypto._notes: Per-position cap for crypto signals (3.4.1). Wider spreads + 24/7 markets mean we size more conservatively than equity. Auto_trader detects crypto symbols via monitoring.crypto_adapter.is_crypto_symbol and uses this cap instead of auto_trade.max_position_usd. Set 0 to fall back to the default (500).

auto_trade._notes_stops: 6.1.1/6.1.2 — Initial-stop policy lives under top-level `stops` (see below), not here. Legacy `stop_loss_atr_multiple` is still honored when set for backward-compat with Phase 4.6 trend strategies.
auto_trade._notes: enabled=false → process_signals is never called. enabled=true + dry_run=true → logs would-be orders without submitting. enabled=true + dry_run=false → REAL Alpaca paper orders. Eligibility = realized_stats_gate (when enabled and enough recent fresh closes, block if recent win-rate or avg return is below thresholds) AND (closed_outcomes >= min_outcomes) AND (mean_ret_pct >= min_mean_ret_pct) AND (sharpe_ish >= min_sharpe_ish). Strategies with `grace_period: true` in their TRACKED_STRATEGIES declaration bypass the min_outcomes gate while they accumulate data, and their max_position_usd is reduced by grace_period_size_multiplier (default 0.25 → quarter size). Once n >= min_outcomes the strategy graduates and normal thresholds apply. Cool-down: when a strategy's last cool_down_losers closed outcomes were ALL losers, pause new entries for cool_down_days trading days. Set cool_down_losers=0 to disable. Earnings veto: skip entries on a symbol within earnings_veto_days trading days of its next earnings event (populated by monitoring.earnings_calendar). Set to 0 to disable. Negative-sentiment veto: when veto_negative_sentiment=true, skip entries on symbols with >= negative_sentiment_threshold negative-sentiment news items in the last negative_sentiment_window_hours hours (requires news.sentiment populated by news_fetcher). Live-strategies routing: each strategy_id listed in live_strategies submits to a separate `alpaca_live` credentials section (paper=false). Empty list (default) keeps every strategy on paper. Adding a strategy here requires an alpaca_live section in credentials.json — without it, those signals SKIP_LIVE_CREDS_MISSING and never submit. Intraday master switch (5.2.3): intraday_enabled=false → auto_trader_intraday.process_intraday short-circuits with DISABLED_INTRADAY; intraday_fires still records signals (observe-only). intraday_enabled=true → intraday signals at each interval in intraday_intervals (default ['15m']) are processed through the same eligibility/sizing/regime pipeline as EOD signals.

stops._notes: 6.1.1 / 6.1.2 / 6.1.3 — Initial-stop policy. Stops at entry are computed as entry_price - (atr_multiplier × ATR_n) for longs (mirrored for shorts). Default ATR period is 14, default multiplier is 2.5. by_class.{strategy_class}.atr_multiplier lets a whole class override the default (6.1.2 sets mean_reversion → 2.0). per_strategy.{strategy_id}.atr_multiplier overrides everything else for a single strategy. fixed_percent_fallback (e.g. 0.05 = 5%) supplies a fallback stop when ATR can't be computed (<14 bars of history). 6.1.3 — set regime_aware=true to scale k by the current market regime: k_effective = k_base × regime_multipliers[current_regime]. Multiplier clamped to [0.7, 1.5] so a misconfigured table can't blow up the stop band. Setting any value to 0 / null at a level lets the next-level default win.

kelly._notes: 6.2.2 — Fractional Kelly sizing tier. Activated per-strategy by setting auto_trade.sizing_method='kelly_quarter'. fraction_of_kelly (default 0.25 = ¼ Kelly) is multiplied against the raw Kelly fraction; code hard-clamps this at 0.5 (½ Kelly) so config typos can never push to full Kelly. max_position_fraction (default 0.05 = 5%) caps any single position as a fraction of portfolio. min_samples (default 50) is the closed-outcome guard — below it, sizing falls back to the tiered (3.2.1) tier. Ross can promote a strategy to fraction_of_kelly=0.5 after it accumulates 200+ closed outcomes.
kelly._notes_pf_size_up: Sprint-1 M4 — expectancy-tiered size-up. A strategy whose measured profit factor exceeds pf_threshold (2.0) gets boosted_max_position_fraction (0.15) instead of the base max_position_fraction (0.10), so proven high-edge EOD winners (3-bar-low PF 2.92, 4bar-reversal PF 3.31) deploy more of the idle capital. fraction_of_kelly stays at 0.25 (NOT raised — conservative posture held until stop effectiveness is confirmed via M1 MFE/MAE). The boost is hard-clamped in code to 0.20. Set enabled=false to disable.

trailing_stop._notes: 6.4 — Global trailing-stop policy. method one of {atr_trail, chandelier, percent_trail}. atr_trail: stop = highest_high_since_entry − (multiplier × ATR_14), ratcheting up only (never loosens). multiplier 3.0 gives the trade room above the tighter 2.5× ATR initial stop. Floored against the entry-time ATR stop so it can never slip below the initial protective level. Per-strategy overrides go in that strategy's TRACKED_STRATEGIES declaration under `trailing_stop`. Requires a bars_fetcher in the live path (wired in auto_trader.process_signals). Set method to null to disable.

intraday._notes: Sprint-1 M4 — intraday position floor. After the intraday size multiplier (0.5) shrinks the notional, it is floored to this value so an intraday entry can afford >=1 share of liquid names (SPY ~$590-760, QQQ ~$500, NVDA ~$140). Applies to intraday entries only (bar_interval != '1d'); EOD sizing is untouched. Stays well within risk.max_position_usd ($10k) and, at risk.max_open_positions (12), caps intraday exposure far below the idle cash buffer. Set to 0 to disable the intraday-specific floor.

max_loss_cap._notes: Sprint-3 M10 — trend loser cap. A HARD per-position max-loss floor for the trend book (trend-donchian-breakout-20, trend-ma-cross-20-50). When an open position's unrealised loss from ENTRY reaches max_loss_pct (default 8.0 = -8%), it is force-closed (broker sell + outcome close with exit_reason='max_loss_cap') even though the ATR trailing stop hasn't tripped — the trailing stop only ratchets DOWN from the high, so a position that gaps/bleeds straight off entry (ENPH -16%, AVGO -16% the week of 2026-06-03) never engages it and bled past any sane single-name loss. This cap bounds the tail WITHOUT touching the strategy's entry/exit logic or weakening any risk.* limit, the paper gate, or the kill switch — it only ADDS a tighter exit. Resolution mirrors time_stop/trailing_stop: a per-strategy `max_loss_cap` declaration in TREND_DECLARATIONS wins, then this global block. Runs AFTER the trailing + time-stop passes, so either of those wins when it trips first. Set max_loss_pct to 0/null (here or per-strategy) to disable. Default chosen conservatively at -8% (tighter than the worst observed -16% blowups, looser than the ~7.5% a 2.5xATR trail implies on a 3%-ATR name, so it only catches genuine tail bleed).

notes: These are non-secret settings. Edit freely.
